updates.title: Anthropic Pauses Agent SDK Billing Change on the Day It Was Set to Launch
updates.summary: Anthropic reversed course on its May 13 billing overhaul, pausing the separation of Agent SDK usage from standard subscription limits on the exact day it was scheduled to take effect. The planned change would have moved Agent SDK, claude -p, and third-party app usage into a separate monthly credit pool ($20 for Pro, $100 for Max 5x, $200 for Max 20x). Anthropic told users 'nothing changes for now' and confirmed in its Help Center that the change is no longer happening, citing a need to better align the plan with actual usage patterns.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed directly via Anthropic Help Center update and independently reported by The New Stack, The-Decoder, and multiple developer blogs on June 15. Widely corroborated — high confidence. The 'price war with OpenAI' framing in some sources is analyst speculation, not stated by Anthropic.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.178 — Granular Permission Syntax, Nested Skill Directories, Smarter Subagent Spawning
updates.summary: Claude Code version 2.1.178 introduces Tool(param:value) syntax for permission rules, allowing fine-grained control by matching a tool's input parameters (e.g., Agent(model:opus) to block Opus subagents). Nested .claude/ skill directories now load automatically when working on files within them, with name-clash resolution via <dir>:<name> notation. The release also improves the workflow prompt keyword trigger (now requires explicit phrases like 'run a workflow'), upgrades /doctor with a cleaner flat-tree layout, and fixes extensive bugs including OAuth failures, out-of-memory crashes, and broken background session states.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from the official Claude Code changelog at code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog — version number v2.1.178 and all feature/fix descriptions are verified from that primary source.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.179 — Mid-Stream Connection Stability, WSL2 Scrolling Fix, Linux Sandbox Fix
updates.summary: Claude Code version 2.1.179 resolves several platform-specific stability and UX regressions. Partial responses are now preserved on mid-stream connection drops instead of showing raw errors, and the spinner no longer gets stuck at 'running tool'. Mouse-wheel scrolling in WSL2 under Windows Terminal and VS Code (broken since 2.1.172) is restored. A critical Linux sandbox bug where glob matching over large directory trees made the Bash tool description enormous — and sessions unusable — is also fixed.
updates.accuracy_notes: Sourced directly from the official Claude Code changelog at code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog — version v2.1.179 and all fixes confirmed from that primary source. Date confirmed as June 16, 2026.

updates.title: Claude API: Sonnet 4 & Opus 4 Retired; Refusal Requests No Longer Billed; Advisor Tool Gets max_tokens
updates.summary: Three API changes took effect June 15: (1) Claude Sonnet 4 (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) and Claude Opus 4 (claude-opus-4-20250514) were retired from the API as of 9 AM PT — APIs now return errors; recommended migrations are to Sonnet 4.6 and Opus 4.8 respectively. (2) API requests returning stop_reason: 'refusal' with no generated output are no longer billed; an opt-in 'fallbacks' parameter re-runs refused requests on an alternative model. (3) The advisor tool now supports a max_tokens parameter to cap per-call output, reducing latency and cost for long-horizon agentic workloads.
updates.accuracy_notes: Model deprecation dates confirmed across Anthropic's official deprecation docs and Make.com/developer partner documentation. Refusal billing change confirmed by multiple secondary sources citing release notes. Advisor tool max_tokens confirmed via search. All three changes appear in the docs.anthropic.com release notes for June 15.
